summary: No-ship: the driver can still pass without proving rate-limit-only tolerance or billing, and cleanup is not guaranteed on thrown paths.

findings.title: Overbroad tolerated path can swallow real upstream failures as rate limits
findings.recommendation: Only classify explicit rate-limit evidence as tolerated, e.g. HTTP 429 or Anthropic error.type === 'rate_limit_error'. Treat other error bodies and 5xx HTTP statuses as failures, and avoid tolerating empty CLI output unless the captured failure proves rate limiting.

findings.title: Billing proof can be skipped while the live gate still passes
findings.recommendation: Make unreadable billing breakdown a gate failure for this proof driver, or otherwise assert before/after growth through a reliable billing endpoint. The invariant requires verifying billedAfter > billedBefore.

findings.title: Minted key, deny-list entry, and temp workdirs are not cleaned up on every path
findings.recommendation: Wrap the post-mint test body in try/finally and run revoke, deny-list delete, and rmSync cleanup from the finally block, guarding unset IDs and temp paths.

findings.title: Known-limitation deny-listed probe can be printed as OK
findings.recommendation: Do not call ok() for the deny-listed known-limitation beat. Print a visibly distinct resolved/known-limitation diagnostic that does not look like an asserted PASS/OK gate beat.
